summary: Coder v4 honestly closes all 5 findings from tester v3. The MAJOR `Pattern B regex restricts to public` toothlessness is genuinely fixed: regex now matches `public|protected`, and the fix is pinned by 3 explicit regex assertions plus 6 helper-driven synthetic tests (3 IsOrphanMethod + 3 StripCommentsAndStrings) that drive the orphan-detection logic independently of the live tree. 4 empirical deletion tests confirm each new contract bites when its target mutates: regex narrow → MatchesProtectedDeclaration fails; IsOrphanMethod neutered → Heuristic_OrphanProtectedMethod + 3 strip tests fail; Strip identity → 3 strip tests fail; tracking-name swap → unfiltered cache-hit test fails. C# 2466/2466 green (v3 was 2456; +10 v4 tests). The 4 minor findings (synthetic regression-test gap, comment/string false-positive risk, --coverage flakiness, dead unfiltered constant) are also closed by the same set of changes plus the new pre-Where cache-hit test. No production code changed in v4; the only production-code touchpoint is that the previously-dead `ActionInfoTrackingName` constant is now load-bearing via the new test. One observation noted but not filed: integration of StripCommentsAndStrings into LoadAllCallableSources is not directly pinned (Pattern A has the same architecture gap, so precedent-aligned). Recommend running security analyst next.
